title: GeM Onboarding — Sell to India's Government
tagline: Access ₹2+ lakh crore in annual government procurement with relaxed eligibility for DPIIT-recognised startups.
unlocks: Direct sales to 10,000+ government buyers (Ministries, PSUs, Defence, Railways, Hospitals)
Startup Runway: DPIIT startups exempt from prior experience and turnover requirements
GeM Pool: tender notifications across categories directly to your email
Faster payment cycles: government pays in 10-15 days via GeM (vs. 60-90 days in traditional procurement)
Transparent pricing: no middlemen, direct manufacturer-to-government pricing

steps.title: Prepare Your Documents
steps.description: Gather all required documents before starting the registration to avoid mid-process delays.
details: PAN Card of the business entity
GSTIN (GST Identification Number) — mandatory for sellers with turnover > ₹20 lakh
Bank account details (cancelled cheque or bank statement with IFSC)
Aadhaar of the Primary User (owner/authorised signatory)
Certificate of Incorporation / Partnership Deed / GST Certificate as entity proof
DPIIT Recognition Certificate (for Startup Runway access)
Udyam Registration Certificate (for MSME benefits on GeM)
tips: GSTIN is mandatory for most product sellers. Service providers below ₹20 lakh can register without GST.
Have all documents as clear PDFs ready before starting

steps.title: Register as Seller on GeM
steps.description: Create your seller account on the Government e-Marketplace portal.
details: Go to gem.gov.in
Click 'Sign Up' → Select 'Seller'
Enter mobile number + OTP for initial registration
Select your organisation type (Individual / Proprietorship / Partnership / LLP / Private Limited / Public Limited / PSU)
Enter PAN number and verify
Enter GSTIN (if applicable) and verify
Enter Aadhaar number of authorised signatory + OTP verification
Upload relevant business documents (CoI, partnership deed as applicable)
Enter bank account details for payment
tips: Use the mobile number that is linked to your Aadhaar for seamless verification
GeM verifies your PAN and GSTIN from government databases in real-time
Registration on GeM is completely free

steps.title: Activate Startup Runway (DPIIT Startups)
steps.description: Link your DPIIT Recognition Certificate to unlock relaxed eligibility for government procurement.
details: After basic registration, go to 'My Profile' on GeM dashboard
Under 'Certificates', click 'Add Certificate'
Select 'Startup India (DPIIT)' from certificate type
Enter your DPIIT Recognition Number (format: DIPP#########)
Upload DPIIT Recognition Certificate PDF
GeM verifies with Startup India database (usually within 24 hours)
Once verified, your profile shows 'Startup' badge — you're on Startup Runway
tips: Startup Runway removes the requirement for prior year turnover or experience that normal GeM sellers need
Also link Udyam certificate if you have it — additional MSME benefits apply
Startups can bid for items even if they haven't sold that product to anyone before

steps.title: Create Product/Service Catalog
steps.description: List your products or services on GeM with proper categorization, pricing, and specifications.
details: Go to 'Products/Services' → 'Add Product' or 'Add Service'
For products: search and select the correct product category and specification template
Fill all mandatory specifications (dimensions, materials, technical specs)
Upload product images (minimum 4 images: front, back, sides, usage — white background preferred)
Set your price (inclusive of GST)
Select HSN/SAC code for the product/service
Set delivery timelines and serviceability (Pan-India or specific states)
For services: define scope, deliverables, SLAs, and pricing model (fixed / per unit / per day)
tips: Products with no specification template: raise a 'New Product Request' to GeM team (take 7-10 days to create new template)
Pricing on GeM is visible to all buyers — price competitively. Buyers compare multiple sellers.
For innovative startup products with no comparable: use the 'Direct Purchase' narrative highlighting unique capabilities

steps.title: Participate in Bids and Direct Purchases
steps.description: Respond to bid invitations from government buyers and optimize for direct purchase visibility.
details: Direct Purchase (L1 buying): For purchases up to ₹25,000 per item — buyer chooses lowest price seller directly
L1 buying up to ₹5 lakh: Buyer gets 3 quotes from eligible sellers; lowest wins
Reverse Auction / Bid: For large purchases — submit your best bid by deadline
Set up email notifications for bids in your categories at 'My Bids' → 'Notifications'
Respond to custom requirements ('Demand') from buyers who can't find standard products
Target Ministry buyers, State PSUs, Central PSUs, Defence (separate buyer portal)
Track GeM Analytical Dashboard for your category demand volumes
tips: D-class items (₹<25,000): Focus on price — buyers auto-pick lowest price without comparing
For complex/technical products: write strong product descriptions explaining ROI to non-technical government buyers
Build a relationship with the GeM SPOCs (Single Point of Contact) in target ministries for custom deals

steps.title: Fulfill Orders and Get Paid
steps.description: Complete orders, submit invoices on GeM, and receive payment within 10-15 days.
details: When order received: log in to GeM, accept order within 24 hours
Deliver as per committed timeline — update tracking on GeM portal
After delivery: upload invoice on GeM with HSN code, GST breakdown, delivery proof
Buyer inspects and accepts delivery on GeM portal
Payment processed within 10 working days via PFMS (Public Financial Management System)
For late payment beyond 45 days: MSME Protection Act — you can charge compound interest at 3x bank rate
tips: GeM payment is direct bank transfer — track on GeM dashboard under 'Payments'
If payment delayed: raise complaint on GeM grievance portal → escalates to buyer's DDO
Build GeM order history — higher order count improves your ranking in search results

faqs.q: Do I need GST registration for GeM?
faqs.a: For most product sales, yes. Service providers with turnover < ₹20 lakh are exempt. Composite scheme taxpayers can also sell on GeM.

faqs.q: What are the most in-demand categories on GeM for startups?
faqs.a: Software/SaaS licenses, IT hardware, office supplies, furniture, training services, cybersecurity solutions, clean energy products, medical supplies, and consultancy services.

faqs.q: Can I list my SaaS product on GeM?
faqs.a: Yes! SaaS and cloud services are listed under the 'Software (as a Service)' and 'IT Services' categories. Many government departments are actively procuring SaaS on GeM.

faqs.q: Is pricing on GeM my problem or theirs?
faqs.a: GeM uses L1 pricing (lowest gets the order) for most purchases. However, for specialized/unique products, government buyers can negotiate and justify higher prices.
